body:
## Reproduction

```sh
mkdir /tmp/agent-peer-repro
cd /tmp/agent-peer-repro
bun init -y
bun add @link-assistant/agent@0.25.0
```

With Bun 1.3.14 this emits:

```text
warn: incorrect peer dependency "solid-js@1.9.14"
```

The same warning is emitted by Formal AI's current Agent CLI E2E install and release-image build.

## Root cause

The resolved dependency graph contains:

- `@link-assistant/agent@0.25.0` -> `solid-js: ^1.9.10`
- `@link-assistant/agent@0.25.0` -> `@opentui/solid: ^0.1.79`, currently resolving to 0.1.107
- `@opentui/solid@0.1.107` -> exact peer `solid-js: 1.9.12`
- Bun resolves the Agent range to current `solid-js@1.9.14`

Thus Agent's range permits a version rejected by the exact OpenTUI peer.

## Suggested fix

Align the direct `solid-js` version with the selected `@opentui/solid` peer, or upgrade/pin the OpenTUI pair to a release whose peer range accepts the selected Solid version. Add a clean `bun install` assertion to release CI so unexpected peer warnings fail rather than becoming baseline noise.

## Workaround

Consumers can override/pin `solid-js` to 1.9.12 while Agent uses OpenTUI 0.1.107, but the published package should carry a compatible dependency set so global installs remain warning-free.

Discovered during link-assistant/formal-ai#730.
title: Agent 0.25.0 install warns about incompatible solid-js peer dependency
